factors.summary: Regulatory changes are not significantly impacting the company at this time.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to our critical accounting estimates for the three months ended June 30, 2017.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is stable, with no significant costs or operational constraints reported.

evidence.text: We believe that we have the liquidity to make our future tax payments.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs are not significantly affecting the company at this time.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to our critical accounting estimates for the three months ended June 30, 2017.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is stable, with no significant legal risks reported.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to the risk factors affecting our business, financial condition or future results from those set forth in Item 1A. 'Risk Factors' in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2016.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on enhancing customer experience through new technologies and product improvements.

evidence.text: We plan to expand the premium class offering on our Airbus fleet beginning in 2018 and have our entire fleet equipped with high-speed satellite Wi-Fi by early 2020.

factors.summary: Digital automation is being adopted through initiatives like free Wi-Fi and entertainment systems, enhancing the customer experience.

evidence.text: Our onboard Free Chat service and free entertainment will be added to Airbus flights in August 2017. We also plan to expand the premium class offering on our Airbus fleet beginning in 2018 and have our entire fleet equipped with high-speed satellite Wi-Fi by early 2020.

factors.summary: New products include enhanced inflight entertainment, connectivity, and meal pre-selection, which are gaining traction.

evidence.text: This year, Alaska First Class guests will be able to pre-select meals before they fly. Alaska’s main cabin guests will also be able to pre-pay for their meals in advance in 2018, with Airbus flights soon to follow. Our onboard Free Chat service and free entertainment will be added to Airbus flights in August 2017.

factors.summary: Market share is increasing, particularly in the West Coast and international markets, driven by the Virgin America integration.

evidence.text: We completed the acquisition of Virgin America on December 14, 2016, positioning us as the fifth largest airline in the U.S. with a unique ability to serve West Coast travelers. The acquisition of Virgin America provides a platform for growth of our low-fare, premium product, a powerful West Coast network for our guests and enhanced international partnerships.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intensifying, with the company focusing on differentiation through service quality, customer experience, and strategic partnerships.

evidence.text: Our product, our operation, our low-cost structure, our engaged employees, our award-winning service, and our award-winning Mileage Plan™ and Elevate® programs, combined with our strong balance sheet, give us the ability to compete effectively in our markets.

factors.summary: Differentiation is a key competitive advantage, with a focus on customer experience, service quality, and unique brand elements from Virgin America.

evidence.text: We will adopt certain aspects of Virgin America’s brand elements, including enhanced inflight connectivity, inflight entertainment content, mood lighting, music, and the relentless desire to make flying a different experience for guests.

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving, with the company expanding its network and customer base through new routes and partnerships.

evidence.text: We have announced approximately 40 new markets since the acquisition of Virgin America as we begin to realize the network and revenue synergies from bringing our two airlines together.

factors.summary: Volume trends are positive, with increases in fuel consumption, passenger revenue, and load factors, indicating higher business activity and efficiency.

evidence.text: Fuel gallons consumed increased by 14 million gallons, or 7.5%, in line with the increase in capacity.
Mainline passenger revenue increased due to higher capacity from the new routes we have added over the past twelve months.
Load factor 86.8% for the second quarter of 2017, compared to 84.9% in the second quarter of 2016. Load factor 87.3% for Mainline, compared to 85.6% in the second quarter of 2016.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is improving across the company, driven by higher load factors, increased capacity, and new routes. However, this improvement has led to higher fuel expenses due to increased fuel consumption.

evidence.text: On a Combined Comparative basis, raw fuel expense per gallon for the three months ended June 30, 2017 increased by 13% due to higher West Coast jet fuel prices. Fuel gallons consumed increased by 14 million gallons, or 7.5% , in line with the increase in capacity.
Aircraft Utilization - block hours per day; this represents the average number of hours per day our aircraft are in transit
Aircraft utilization 11.4 for Mainline, compared to 10.8 in the second quarter of 2016. The company expects to grow its combined network capacity in 2017 by approximately 8%

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are rising, creating margin pressure. The company is partially offsetting these increases through pricing.

evidence.text: West Coast jet fuel prices are impacted by both the price of crude oil, as well as refining margins associated with the conversion of crude oil to jet fuel. The increase in raw fuel price per gallon during the second quarter of 2017 was primarily driven by a 6% increase in crude oil prices and an increase in refining margins of 17%, when compared to the prior year.

The $63 million decrease in Combined Comparative pretax profit was primarily driven by a $122 million increase in Mainline fuel expense and $113 million increase in Mainline non-fuel operating expenses, partially offset by a $192 million increase in Mainline passenger revenue. Higher raw fuel prices and an increase in gallons consumed drove the increase in Mainline fuel expense.

Fuel expense $344 million, compared to $201 million in the second quarter of 2016. Economic fuel cost per gallon increased by 11.8%. The company is partially offsetting these increases through pricing.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed margin performance. While operating margins are under pressure due to rising fuel and labor costs, as well as merger-related expenses, there are signs of improvement with expanding margins driven by revenue growth and cost management.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel expense increased $143 million, or 71% compared to the second quarter of 2016. We recorded special items of $24 million for merger-related costs associated with our acquisition of Virgin America in the second quarter of 2017.
Aircraft fuel expense increased $143 million, or 71% compared to the second quarter of 2016. Non-fuel operating expenses increased by $107 million, or 9%.

The $63 million decrease in Combined Comparative pretax profit was primarily driven by a $122 million increase in Mainline fuel expense and $113 million increase in Mainline non-fuel operating expenses, partially offset by $192 million increase in Mainline passenger revenue.
Adjusted net income for the second quarter of 2017 was $312 million, compared to $263 million in the second quarter of 2016. CASM excluding fuel and special items increased by 2.1%.
evidence.subsection: COMPARISON OF THREE MONTHS ENDED JUNE 30, 2017 TO THREE MONTHS ENDED JUNE 30, 2016

factors.summary: The factor of organic growth is showing positive trends across the company's segments. The evidence highlights that organic revenue growth is stable and accelerating, driven by higher capacity, PRASM improvements, new routes, and increased unit revenues. These factors contribute to the overall growth and indicate a strong performance in the Mainline passenger revenue.

evidence.text: The increase in operating revenues was primarily driven by higher capacity, higher PRASM and increase in frequent flyer revenue as described above.
Mainline passenger revenue increased due to higher capacity from the new routes we have added over the past twelve months.

On a Combined Comparative basis, Mainline passenger revenue for the second quarter of 2017 increased by 10%, due to a 5.7% increase in capacity, as well as a 3.7% increase in unit revenues combined with higher frequent flyer revenue compared to the combined second quarter of 2016.

factors.summary: The company's pricing power appears to be stable with some positive trends. While PRASM showed slight decreases in some periods, overall revenue growth has been driven by higher capacity and customer demand. Additionally, there are signs of success in raising prices, supported by higher unit revenues, ticket yields, and load factors.

evidence.text: Mainline passenger revenue for the first six months of 2017 increased 44% on a 43.5% increase in capacity, driven primarily by the acquisition of Virgin America, and flat PRASM compared to the same period in 2016.
Mainline passenger revenue increased due to higher capacity from the new routes we have added over the past twelve months.

The increase in PRASM was driven by higher ticket yields and an increase in load factors. The increase in frequent flyer revenue was driven by higher loyalty redemption and companion certificate revenue attributable to promotions during the period.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to multiple factors, including higher wages from increased full-time equivalents (FTEs), growth in business operations, bringing airport ground service positions in-house, pilot contract negotiations, and overall wage adjustments to support business expansion.

evidence.text: On a Combined Comparative basis, wages increased 17% with a 14% increase in FTEs. The increase in FTEs is attributable to the growth in our business, as well as bringing certain airport ground service positions in-house that were previously reflected in our Contracted services expense.
Non-fuel operating expenses increased due to higher wages to support our growth and higher other operating expenses as described above.
We expect this contract will increase our annual wages and benefits costs by approximately $140 million.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction initiatives, particularly in efficiency programs and fuel management, which have led to a decrease in variable incentive pay. However, the impact of these programs on accounting estimates remains uncertain.

evidence.text: Variable incentive pay decreased by $5 million , or 16% , during the second quarter of 2017 compared to the same period in 2016. On a Combined Comparative basis, variable incentive pay decreased by $13 million , or 33% . This decrease is primarily due to expectations of lower performance-based pay as compared to the prior year based on how we are tracking in relation to our current year's goals.
There have been no material changes to our critical accounting estimates for the three months ended June 30, 2017.

We believe that even a small reduction in non-fuel operating costs can lead to a significant improvement in operating results. The company is focused on capturing synergies from the Virgin America integration.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving across the company, supported by higher load factors, increased revenue per available seat mile, and operational efficiency. Non-fuel expenses are growing slower than revenue, indicating productivity gains.

evidence.text: On a Combined Comparative basis, non-fuel operating expenses increased by 7%, while operating revenues increased by 36%
Load Factor - RPMs as a percentage of ASMs; represents the number of available seats that were filled with paying passengers.
Load factor 86.8% for the second quarter of 2017, compared to 84.9% in the second quarter of 2016.
CASM excluding fuel and special items increased by 2.1%

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, indicating confidence in future performance. However, there are concerns about potential margin compression due to expected increases in fuel costs.

evidence.text: We currently expect our economic fuel price per gallon to be higher in the third quarter of 2017 compared to the third quarter of 2016 due to our current estimate of higher crude prices and higher refining margins.

We typically generate positive cash flows from operations and expect to use that cash flow to purchase aircraft and capital equipment, make scheduled debt payments and to return capital to shareholders.

We currently expect to receive a Single Operating Certificate ('SOC') in early 2018. We also expect to be on a single Passenger Service System ('PSS') in the second quarter of 2018, bringing forward approximately $20 million of revenue synergies into 2018.

factors.summary: Management confidence appears mixed. While management is cautious due to rising merger-related costs and integration challenges, they demonstrate confidence through continued investment in capacity and capital expenditures, as well as share buybacks and focus on long-term strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: We expect to incur merger-related costs for the remainder of 2017 through 2019. The first six months of 2017 also include $9 million of ratification bonus expense in connection with the agreement reached with Horizon's pilots.

Cash used in investing activities was $971 million during the first six months of 2017, compared to $590 million during the same period of 2016. Our capital expenditures were $512 million in the first six months of 2017, an increase of $172 million compared to the six months ended June 30, 2016.

During the second quarter of 2017, we paid cash dividends of $37 million and repurchased 256,680 shares for $22 million. The company is investing in new routes, aircraft, and customer experience improvements.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on fleet modernization, capacity expansion, and enhancing customer experience through integration of Virgin America. While the strategy is consistent across subsections, there are differing focuses on operational efficiency and fleet management, such as aircraft retirements and lease arrangements, alongside customer experience improvements and brand integration.

evidence.text: For the full year, we expect aircraft rent to be lower than in 2016 as we retire four more leased B737-400 aircraft and realize the full year benefit of lower rent expense on certain leases at Virgin America. We currently expect wages and benefits to increase for the full year to support the general growth of the business and staff certain airport ground service positions that we began performing internally.
For future firm orders, and if we exercise our options for additional deliveries, we may finance the aircraft through internally generated cash, long-term debt or lease arrangements.

The combined airline will adopt Alaska’s name and logo, retiring the Virgin America name sometime in 2019. Over the next few years, we will focus on enhancing our guest experience and will adopt certain aspects of Virgin America’s brand elements.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks primarily related to rising fuel costs and the integration challenges of Virgin America. While management is actively addressing these issues, particularly focusing on cost synergies, the increased fuel expenses and merger-related costs highlight the current challenges.

evidence.text: We currently expect our economic fuel price per gallon to be higher in the third quarter of 2017 compared to the third quarter of 2016 due to our current estimate of higher crude prices and higher refining margins. We recorded special items of $24 million for merger-related costs associated with our acquisition of Virgin America in the second quarter of 2017.

For the first six months of 2017, net cash provided by operating activities was $1.1 billion, compared to $899 million during the same period in 2016. The $185 million increase in our operating cash flows is primarily attributable to the larger build in air traffic liability in 2017 compared to the prior year and the addition of Virgin America. This was partially offset by a decrease in our net income, which was impacted by higher fuel costs and $64 million of merger-related costs.
Fuel expense increased by 45.7% in fuel gallons. The company is focused on integrating Virgin America and achieving cost synergies.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and refining margins are significant factors impacting fuel costs, with economic conditions and labor costs also playing a role. The evidence highlights that fuel expenses are driven by both raw fuel price increases and refining margins, as well as non-fuel costs influenced by wage growth.

evidence.text: West Coast jet fuel prices are impacted by both the price of crude oil, as well as refining margins associated with the conversion of crude oil to jet fuel. The increase in raw fuel price per gallon during the second quarter of 2017 was primarily driven by a 6% increase in crude oil prices and an increase in refining margins of 17%, when compared to the prior year.

Higher raw fuel prices and an increase in gallons consumed drove the increase in Mainline fuel expense. Non-fuel operating expenses increased due to higher wages to support our growth and higher other operating expenses as described above.

Fuel expense increased by 45.7% in fuel gallons. Economic fuel cost per gallon increased by 11.8%. We expect this contract will increase our annual wages and benefits costs by approximately $140 million.

factors.summary: The company faces several key catalysts, including the resolution of pilot arbitration which could impact costs, the completion of the Single Operating Certificate and integration of Virgin America's operations to drive revenue synergies, and the addition of new routes and capacity improvements expected to enhance results.

evidence.text: Our current unit cost guidance does not include an estimate for these increases at this time. Guidance will be updated after the arbitration decision.
Mainline passenger revenue increased due to higher capacity from the new routes we have added over the past twelve months.

We currently expect to receive a SOC in early 2018. We also expect to be on a single Passenger Service System ('PSS') in the second quarter of 2018, bringing forward approximately $20 million of revenue synergies into 2018.

factors.summary: Capacity is increasing, load factors are improving, and revenue per available seat mile (RASM) is slightly declining.

evidence.text: Load Factor - RPMs as a percentage of ASMs; represents the number of available seats that were filled with paying passengers.
System-wide capacity increased by 8% in 2017 compared to 2016. Load factor improved to 86.8% from 84.9%. PRASM increased by 3.7% on a Combined Comparative basis.

factors.summary: The company is expanding its route network, which is expected to be accretive. The expansion includes new routes and international partnerships, contributing to higher passenger revenue and capacity growth.

evidence.text: The increase in operating revenues was primarily driven by higher capacity, higher PRASM and increase in frequent flyer revenue as described above.
Mainline passenger revenue increased due to higher capacity from the new routes we have added over the past twelve months.

We launched nine new routes during the quarter and announced 10 new routes, including obtaining final approval from the Department of Transportation for three routes to Mexico City. The company expects to grow its combined network capacity in 2017 by approximately 8%.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing rising fuel costs, but its hedging strategy shows mixed results. While the hedging positions are designed to cap crude oil costs and benefit from price declines, mark-to-market adjustments indicate current losses.

evidence.text: Mark-to-market fuel hedge adjustments 2 0.01 (10 ) (0.07 ) (11 ) (0.05 ) GAAP fuel expense $ 344 $ 1.72 $ 201 $ 1.46 $ 278 $ 1.49

All of our current oil positions are call options, which are designed to effectively cap the cost of the crude oil component of our jet fuel purchases. With call options, we benefit from a decline in crude oil prices, as there is no cash outlay other than the premiums we pay to enter into the contracts.
Economic fuel cost per gallon increased by 11.8% in the second quarter of 2017 compared to the same period in 2016. The company does not provide full-year economic fuel estimates due to volatility.

factors.summary: The company anticipates an increase in capital expenditures, driven by various initiatives including fleet expansion, IT investments, aircraft purchases, and technology improvements. This indicates confidence in future growth and expansion plans.

evidence.text: For the full year, we expect other expenses to be higher than in 2016 in line with the trends described above.
Cash used in investing activities was $971 million during the first six months of 2017, compared to $590 million during the same period of 2016.
The company is investing in new routes, aircraft, and customer experience improvements, such as high-speed satellite Wi-Fi by early 2020.

factors.summary: The company's fleet strategy is primarily focused on modernization and efficiency. This includes both the expansion of the fleet with new aircraft orders and the implementation of technological upgrades such as high-speed satellite Wi-Fi. Additionally, the strategy involves maintaining fleet efficiency through scheduled maintenance and potential retirements to optimize operational performance.

evidence.text: Aircraft maintenance increased by $31 million , or 48% , during the second quarter of 2017 compared to the same period in 2016 . On a Combined Comparative basis, aircraft maintenance increased by $14 million , or 17% . This increase was primarily driven by a higher volume of scheduled engine maintenance events as compared to the prior period.
As of June 30, 2017, we have firm orders to purchase or lease 83 aircraft. We also have cancelable purchase commitments for 30 Airbus A320neo with deliveries from 2020 through 2022.

Our onboard Free Chat service and free entertainment will be added to Airbus flights in August 2017. We also plan to expand the premium class offering on our Airbus fleet beginning in 2018 and have our entire fleet equipped with high-speed satellite Wi-Fi by early 2020.

factors.summary: The travel demand mix is favorable, with both business and leisure segments contributing to growth. However, PRASM is slightly declining, and there are signs of higher demand for business and premium classes.

evidence.text: Mainline passenger revenue for the first six months of 2017 increased 44% on a 43.5% increase in capacity, driven primarily by the acquisition of Virgin America, and flat PRASM compared to the same period in 2016.
Load Factor - RPMs as a percentage of ASMs; represents the number of available seats that were filled with paying passengers.

We are focused on enhancing our guest experience and will adopt certain aspects of Virgin America’s brand elements, including enhanced inflight connectivity, inflight entertainment content, mood lighting, music, and the relentless desire to make flying a different experience for guests.

factors.summary: Debt leverage presents a mixed picture with concerns about increasing interest expenses due to debt issued for an acquisition, but overall stability in long-term debt and interest costs is noted.

evidence.text: During the first six months of 2017, we had nonoperating expense of $28 million, compared to income of $4 million in the same period in 2016. On a Combined Comparative basis, nonoperating expense increased by $23 million, primarily due to interest expense on the debt issued in the last six months of 2017 to finance the acquisition of Virgin America.
Long-term debt, net of current portion $2,469 as of June 30, 2017, compared to $2,645 as of December 31, 2016.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns appear stable, with the company maintaining returns through consistent dividend payments and share buybacks. While there are no specific increases or decreases mentioned, the evidence highlights ongoing activities that support shareholder value.

evidence.text: During the first six months of 2017, we made debt payments totaling $159 million and paid dividends totaling $74 million. The shares were purchased pursuant to a $1 billion repurchase plan authorized by the Board of Directors in August 2015.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is showing positive trends, with improvements in operating cash flows and net cash provided by operating activities. While revenue growth and cost management contribute to this improvement, capital expenditures have increased, which may impact cash availability.

evidence.text: Total operating revenues increased $1 billion , or 36% , during the first six months of 2017 compared to the same period in 2016 . On a Combined Comparative basis, total operating revenues increased $222 million , or 6% .

factors.summary: Labor relations are generally stable across the company, with no active disputes or strike risks. However, there are ongoing negotiations with pilots that have not yet been resolved, which may indicate potential future issues.

evidence.text: The second quarter of 2017 also includes $9 million of ratification bonus expense in connection with the agreement reached with Horizon's pilots.
Non-fuel operating expenses increased due to higher wages to support our growth and higher other operating expenses as described above.

We have been in negotiations with our pilots on an agreement. We were not able to come to an agreement during mediation, so Alaska and the Air Line Pilot Association will be presenting their positions to an arbitration panel in the third quarter. The decision from the arbitrator will be final and we believe a decision will come some time in the fourth quarter of 2017.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are stable, which is supporting business performance and allowing for positive cash flows from operations. This stability is also reflected in the increase in operating revenues.

evidence.text: The increase in operating revenues was primarily driven by higher capacity, higher PRASM and increase in frequent flyer revenue as described above.

We typically generate positive cash flows from operations and expect to use that cash flow to purchase aircraft and capital equipment, make scheduled debt payments and to return capital to shareholders.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rates on the company is mixed. While interest rates have led to increased nonoperating expenses due to debt issued for the acquisition, they are not currently significantly affecting the company's critical accounting estimates.

evidence.text: During the first six months of 2017 , we had nonoperating expense of $28 million , compared to income of $4 million in the same period in 2016 . factors.summary: The company is focusing on talent retention, with some evidence of increased costs and initiatives to attract and retain top employees, particularly pilots. However, there is also a note of decreased variable incentive pay, which may indicate potential challenges in maintaining retention efforts.

evidence.text: Talent retention is a focus, with costs increasing to retain employees.

This decrease is primarily due to expectations of lower performance-based pay as compared to the prior year based on how we are tracking in relation to our current year's goals.
There have been no material changes to our critical accounting estimates for the three months ended June 30, 2017.

We entered into an agreement with the International Brotherhood of Teamsters to amend the eight-year contract with Horizon's pilots, which will provide Horizon the ability to attract and retain the best pilots in the regional industry.

factors.summary: The analysis of geopolitical risks across different subsections of the SEC filing indicates a mixed perspective. evidence.text: No specific information on geopolitical risks was found in the text.

factors.summary: The company experiences foreign exchange exposure impacting its operating expenses, which increased significantly due to various cost drivers. However, critical accounting estimates remain stable, indicating no significant foreign exchange impact on reported results.

evidence.text: Other operating expenses increased by $59 million , or 73% , during the second quarter of 2017 compared to the same period in 2016 . On a Combined Comparative basis, other operating expenses increased by $34 million , or 32% . The increase is primarily due to additional costs incurred for crew hotel, training, travel, IT and passenger remuneration costs. These increases were driven by the growth in our business and increased costs from irregular operations during the quarter.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting favorably, with both Mainline passenger revenue and frequent flyer programs contributing positively. Higher-margin Mainline operations and ancillary revenues are driving this favorable shift.

evidence.text: Mainline passenger revenue increased due to higher capacity from the new routes we have added over the past twelve months.

Frequent flyer revenue contributed $20 million of the increase, primarily driven by more miles sold to our non-airline partners in the Mileage Plan program. The remainder of the increase was due to higher ancillary revenues.

evidence.text: Mainline pretax profit was $705 million in the first six months of 2017, compared to $670 million in the same period in 2016. Pretax profit for Alaska Regional was $20 million in the first six months of 2017, compared to $37 million in the first six months of 2016.

The acquisition of Virgin America provides a platform for growth of our low-fare, premium product, a powerful West Coast network for our guests and enhanced international partnerships. factors.summary: The inventory levels appear to be stable, as indicated by the absence of significant changes. However, the lack of explicit discussion in the overview section suggests that inventory management might not be a primary focus area for the company.
